summary: Energy flexibility modelling software for developers and utilities.
editor_note: Energy modelling is more valuable when it traces every interval instead of hiding a project behind spreadsheet assumptions. Gridcog says 16,000+ projects across 40+ markets use its platform for generation, storage, flexible load, tariffs, and market participation. Unlike static feasibility tools, it is a decision model for hybrid assets. The risk is converting strategic investors into repeat commercial channels.

product_evidence.claim: The official site positions Gridcog for large-scale asset developers, consultants, e-mobility teams, and energy majors.
The product page says Gridcog helps evaluate utility-scale hybrid and co-located projects for storage, renewables, flexible load, and market participation.
The funding post says Gridcog has been used to model more than 16,000 energy projects across more than 40 markets.

market_context.primary_user: Energy developers, utilities, consultants, e-mobility teams, and energy majors assessing storage, renewables, and flexible-load projects.
market_context.differentiation: Gridcog emphasizes transparent interval-level modelling and optimization for hybrid energy assets rather than black-box feasibility spreadsheets.
market_context.why_now: More renewable generation, storage, and flexible demand are making project value depend on market rules, tariffs, constraints, and co-location choices.

risks.claim: The falsifiable risk is whether Gridcog can scale market coverage and implementation support quickly enough for global developers with local tariff and grid-rule needs.
risks.basis: VentureDex assessment from the official product site and Series A announcement.
